strengths: Effective self-directed learning through research and selection of appropriate training routines
Hands-on experimentation with bench-press variations to reinforce practical skill development
Reflective adjustments to deadlift technique based on performance analysis enhancing safety and efficacy
Rigorous data tracking of training metrics demonstrating use of analytical ATL skills
Use of video analysis for technical refinement showing strong application of multimedia ATL tools
Collaborative learning evident in soliciting feedback from coaches and peers
weaknesses: Does not specify how peer or coach feedback concretely influenced adjustments to technique or planning
Analysis of training data lacks a defined method (e.g., trend graphs or statistical summaries)
Periodization strategy is described but cycle lengths and recovery phases are not clearly delineated
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: **The student applies a broad range of ATL skillsâresearch, practical experimentation, reflective critique, data analysis, multimedia tools and collaborationâto advance both learning and product goals.** To deepen this application, they should detail how external feedback shaped their practice, formalize their dataâanalysis procedures, and articulate precise periodization cycles to optimise progression and recovery.

strengths: Detailed and measurable success criteria covering capacity, technique, safety and consistency
Realistic training plan aligned with football schedule demonstrating balanced resource management
Clear, specific learning goals (80 kg bench press and 150 kg deadlift) providing strong clarity
Thoughtful inclusion of scientific assessment methods (1RM, RM) for objective progress tracking
Meaningful connection to personal growth and self-discovery adding depth to the learning goal
weaknesses: Lacks a clear timeline sequencing each success criterion and phase of the plan
Does not specify required resources (e.g., coaching sessions, equipment availability) for each stage
Testing intervals are not defined; needs scheduled assessment points (e.g., every 4 weeks)
Personal interest connection would benefit from concrete examples of how the project relates to life or career goals
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: **The planning section excels in defining precise, measurable goals and comprehensive success criteria**, and the training routine is thoughtfully integrated with existing commitments. **To elevate the feasibility and trackability of the plan**, the student should introduce a detailed timeline, map out required resources at each stage, establish regular assessment intervals, and illustrate the personal relevance with specific anecdotes or scenarios.

strengths: Deep self-awareness demonstrated through reflection on personal growth, discipline and broader life skills
Thoughtful evaluation of which training strategies were most and least effective
Strong linkage of persistence and determination to transferable life competencies
Insightful reflection on the overall impact of the project on learning and personal development
weaknesses: Evaluation lacks quantitative evidence such as percentage increases in 1RM or rep-count improvements
Technique consistency claims are not supported by specific coach feedback or recorded assessment results
Safety reflections would benefit from documented injury-prevention data or incident logs
Progress and consistency assertions need visual or tabulated summaries (e.g., training-log excerpts or progress graphs)
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: **The reflection is rich in selfâawareness and connects the project to personal and transferable skills,** but the evaluative claims would gain credibility through concrete data. Incorporating quantitative performance metrics, documented feedback records, and visualised progress logs will provide the evidence needed to substantiate the reflective insights.

annotations.comment: Your defined success criteria are detailed and measurable, covering capacity, technique, safety, and consistency. This comprehensive approach sets clear benchmarks for evaluating progress.
Aligning your push-pull-leg routine with your football schedule demonstrates a realistic and balanced plan for achieving the product. Well-considered resource management.
To enhance your plan, sequence the criteria with a timeline and specify resources (e.g., coaching, equipment) to ensure feasibility and tracking of each phase.
Your reflection on personal growth and discipline shows deep self-awareness and impact on learning, aligning well with reflective objectives.
Identifying which strategies were most and least effective demonstrates thoughtful evaluation. Strengthen this by citing specific examples of adjustments made.
Seeking feedback from coaches and peers shows collaborative learning. To deepen this, specify how peer input impacted your technique or planning.
You set specific, measurable learning goals of 80 kg bench press and 150 kg deadlift, which clearly define the intended product and success criteria. Strong clarity in your learning objective.

You connect your strength training project to personal growth and self-discovery, providing depth to your learning goal. Consider adding concrete examples of personal relevance to further strengthen this connection.
Your inclusion of scientific methods (1RM, RM) shows thoughtful planning of objective assessments. Consider specifying testing intervals (e.g., every 4 weeks) for clarity.
Your self-directed learning is evident in how you researched and selected appropriate routines, demonstrating effective ATL skill application for your learning goal.
Prioritizing practical application and experimenting with bench press variations shows strong hands-on learning. This direct practice supports your learning objective effectively.
Your reflective adjustments to deadlift technique after analysis illustrate effective use of reflective ATL skills, enhancing safety and performance.
Your evaluation rates weightlifting capacity clearly, but adding quantitative data (e.g., rep counts or % increase in 1RM) would provide stronger evidence.
You note consistent technique, but referencing specific technique check results or coach feedback would substantiate your evaluation.
You apply data analysis ATL skills by tracking detailed training metrics. To improve, outline how you will analyze trends (e.g., graphs, statistical measures).
Focusing on technical refinement through video analysis demonstrates strong application of ATL skills for the product, boosting safety and efficiency.
Your training periodization strategy balances load and recovery effectively. Consider detailing cycle lengths to better manage progression and prevent plateau.
Safety evaluation is thorough; consider documenting any incidence logs or injury prevention metrics to support your rating.
You highlight consistency and progress well; including a brief summary of your training log or progress graph would strengthen your evaluation.
Linking persistence and determination to broader life skills highlights the transfer of learning. Excellent integration of reflection with personal development.
